REPLY TO PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING INVOLUNTARY DISSOULTION PROCEEDINGS AND APPOINTING APPRAISERS TO ASCERTAIN VALUE OF PLTF'S STOCK FILED BY DEFENDANT SRT AUTOMOTIVE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT SRT AUTOMOTIVE, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING INVOLUNTARY DISSOLUTION PROCEEDINGS AND APPOINTING APPRAISERS TO ASCERTAIN VALUE OF PLAINTIFF'S STOCK (CORP. CODE. 2000). THIS ACTION IS STAYED UPON DEFENDANT'S POSTING OF A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $65,000. UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE, THE COURT WILL APPOINT THREE DISINTERESTED APPRAISERS TO SET THE FAIR VALUE OF PLAINTIFF'S SHARES CONSISTENT WITH CORPORATION CODE SECTION 2000(A) BY THE FOLLOWING PROCESS: A) WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, EACH SIDE WILL SUBMIT TO THE COURT AND PERSONALLY SERVE THE OTHER SIDE WITH THREE NAMES OF DISINTERESTED, QUALIFIED APPRAISERS ALONG WITH RESUMES AND A STATEMENT THAT EACH APPRAISER IS WILLING TO SERVE. B) WITHIN 5 DAYS THEREAFTER, EACH SIDE MAY SUBMIT COMMENTS TO THE COURT CONCERNING THE OTHER SIDE'S NOMINEES. C) THE COURT WILL CHOOSE THREE OF THE SIX NOMINEES TO SERVE. THE CHOSEN APPRAISERS MAY THEN OBTAIN NECESSARY INFORMATION AND ACCESS TO THE BOOKS AND RECORDS FROM THE PARTIES, SO THAT THEY MAY SET THE VALUE AND THEY MAY SCHEDULE ANY HEARINGS OR TIMES TO PRODUCE OR RECEIVE EVIDENCE THAT THEY DEEM APPROPRIATE OR NECESSARY. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Notice And Motion For Order Staying Involuntary Dissolution Proceedings And Appointing Appraisers To Ascertain Value Of Pltf'S Stock (Corp. Code. 2000)
Off Calendar Sep-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. ERIC R. WILDGRUBE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF DENNIS C. BIRKHIMER, TEL. NO. 257-4700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL KAY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF PRITCHARD AND KAY, TEL. NO.(408)920-0500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.
RULING - THE COURT MODIFIED AND ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT S R T AUTOMOTIVE, INC., Motion For Order Staying Involuntary Dissolution Proceedings And Appointing Appraisers To Ascertain Value Of Plaintiff's Stock (Corp. Code. 2000), this action IS STAYED UPON DEFENDANT'S POSTING OF A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $65,000. UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE, THE COURT WILL APPOINT THREE DISINTERESTED APPRAISERS TO SET THE FAIR VALUE OF PLAINTIFF'S SHARES CONSISTENT WITH CORPORATION CODE SECTION 2000(A) BY THE FOLLOWING PROCESS:
A) WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, EACH SIDE WILL SUBMIT TO THE COURT AND PERSONALLY SERVE THE OTHER SIDE WITH THREE NAMES OF DISINTERESTED, QUALIFIED APPRAISERS ALONG WITH RESUMES AND A STATEMENT THAT EACH APPRAISER IS WILLING TO SERVE.
B) WITHIN 5 DAYS THEREAFTER, EACH SIDE MAY SUBMIT COMMENTS TO THE COURT CONCERNING THE OTHER SIDE'S NOMINEES.
C) THE COURT WILL CHOOSE THREE OF THE SIX NOMINEES TO SERVE. THE CHOSEN APPRAISERS MAY THEN OBTAIN NECESSARY INFORMATION AND ACCESS TO THE BOOKS AND RECORDS FROM THE PARTIES, SO THAT THEY MAY SET THE VALUE AND THEY MAY SCHEDULE ANY HEARINGS OR TIMES TO PRODUCE OR RECEIVE EVIDENCE THAT THEY DEEM APPROPRIATE OR NECESSARY. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =301/PJB/LB
